introSection: Black Hole 2000 is a steel roller coaster located in the Future Country section of Seoul Land in Gwacheon-si, Gyeonggi-do, South Korea. Opening in 1990, the coaster is Seoul Land’s first roller coaster to have a corkscrew element and one of the first coasters in South Korea to go upside down.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride has a double corkscrew and six small hills. Track color is usually yellow, but during the layout the track sometimes becomes entirely blue.

fullText:
Black Hole 2000 is a steel roller coaster located in the Future Country section of Seoul Land in Gwacheon-si, Gyeonggi-do, South Korea. Opening in 1990, the coaster is Seoul Land’s first roller coaster to have a corkscrew element and one of the first coasters in South Korea to go upside down.

The ride has a double corkscrew and six small hills. Track color is usually yellow, but during the layout the track sometimes becomes entirely blue.
